intent: Build a simple Python task management library (tasklib). It is not a production system. Its purpose is to validate that the Crafted Dev Agent build pipeline can close a complete dependency chain — from documentation through scaffold to working code — with real merges at each step.
scope_statement:
Build a minimal Python task management library (tasklib) as defined in TRD-TASKLIB, consisting of a documentation set (README, ARCHITECTURE, API reference), a Python package scaffold under src/tasklib/ with models/ and storage/ subpackages, a Task dataclass with TaskStatus enumeration (FR-1 through FR-7), an InMemoryTaskStore (FR-8 through FR-14), a CLI entry point (FR-15 through FR-19), and a package root re-exporting the public API (FR-20). The purpose is to validate the Crafted Dev Agent build pipeline's ability to close a complete dependency chain — docs → scaffold → model → storage → CLI — with real merges at each step.

Ambiguity clarifications:
  - resolve during the PRD process using best practices
  - resolve during the PRD process using best practices
  - resolve during the PRD process using best practices
  - resolve during the PRD process using best practices

prd_plan.title: Documentation Set: README, ARCHITECTURE, and API Reference
Package Scaffold: src/tasklib/ with models/ and storage/ Subpackages
Task Model: TaskStatus Enumeration and Task Dataclass (FR-1 through FR-7)
CLI Entry Point: add, list, and complete Commands (FR-15 through FR-19)
Package Root Public API Re-export (FR-20) and End-to-End Integration Validation

1.title: Add tasklib documentation set: README, ARCHITECTURE, and API reference
1.summary: Creates the three foundational markdown documents that establish the tasklib project's purpose, architecture, and public API contract. README.md describes the library purpose (pipeline validation), package structure (src/tasklib/ with models/ and storage/ subpackages), installation/usage instructions, and a working usage example. ARCHITECTURE.md describes the three-layer design (models → storage → CLI), explains dependency direction rules, and states circular dependencies are not allowed. API.md documents all Task dataclass fields with types and defaults (id: str via uuid4, title: str, description: str default empty, status: TaskStatus default PENDING, created_at/updated_at: datetime), TaskStatus enum members (PENDING, IN_PROGRESS, DONE), InMemoryTaskStore methods with full signatures and return types (add, get, list_all, update, delete), CLI commands (add, list, update, delete) with usage examples, and error behavior (ValueError for invalid status or empty title, KeyError for non-existent task ID).

2.title: Add tasklib Python package scaffold
2.summary: Creates the directory structure and empty __init__.py files for the tasklib package under src/tasklib/, including the models/ and storage/ subpackages. Also includes pyproject.toml with minimal project metadata (name, version 0.1.0, python_requires >=3.10, no external dependencies, console_scripts entry point stub for 'tasklib = tasklib.cli:main'). An empty src/tasklib/cli.py placeholder is included. This PR contains only structural scaffolding — no logic, no model definitions, no store implementation. All __init__.py files are empty placeholders.

3.title: Implement TaskStatus enum and Task dataclass
3.summary: Implements the core domain model in src/tasklib/models/task.py. TaskStatus is a standard-library enum.Enum with members PENDING, IN_PROGRESS, and DONE. Task is a @dataclass with fields: id (str, default_factory=lambda: str(uuid.uuid4())), title (str, required), description (str, default ''), status (TaskStatus, default TaskStatus.PENDING), created_at (datetime, default_factory=datetime.now), updated_at (datetime, default_factory=datetime.now). The Task __post_init__ validates that title is a non-empty string (raises ValueError) and that status is a valid TaskStatus member. The models/__init__.py re-exports Task and TaskStatus. No external dependencies are used.

4.title: Implement InMemoryTaskStore
4.summary: Implements src/tasklib/storage/memory.py containing the InMemoryTaskStore class. The store holds tasks in a private dict[str, Task]. Methods: add(task: Task) -> Task stores and returns the task (raises ValueError if id already exists); get(task_id: str) -> Task retrieves by id (raises KeyError if not found); list_all() -> list[Task] returns all tasks as a list (empty list when store is empty); update(task_id: str, **kwargs) -> Task updates allowed fields (title, description, status) on an existing task, sets updated_at to now, and returns the updated task (raises KeyError if not found, ValueError for invalid field values such as empty title or invalid status); delete(task_id: str) -> None removes a task (raises KeyError if not found). The storage/__init__.py re-exports InMemoryTaskStore.

5.title: Add package root public API re-exports
5.summary: Updates src/tasklib/__init__.py to re-export the complete public API: Task, TaskStatus from tasklib.models, and InMemoryTaskStore from tasklib.storage. Defines __all__ listing all three symbols. This enables users to write 'from tasklib import Task, TaskStatus, InMemoryTaskStore' as documented in README.md and API.md. No new logic is added — this is purely import wiring.

6.title: Implement CLI entry point
6.summary: Implements the CLI in src/tasklib/cli.py using only the standard library (argparse). Provides four subcommands: 'add' (takes --title required and --description optional, creates a task via InMemoryTaskStore, prints task id), 'list' (prints all tasks in a formatted table to stdout, prints 'No tasks found.' message if store is empty), 'update' (takes task_id positional, --title, --description, --status optional kwargs, updates task and prints confirmation), 'delete' (takes task_id positional, deletes task and prints confirmation). A main() function parses args and dispatches to the appropriate handler. Errors (ValueError, KeyError) are caught and printed to stderr with a non-zero exit code. Updates src/tasklib/__init__.py __all__ to also export main from tasklib.cli. The console_scripts entry point 'tasklib = tasklib.cli:main' is already declared in pyproject.toml from PR-2.

7.title: Add end-to-end integration tests
7.summary: Adds integration tests that exercise the complete tasklib pipeline from Task creation through InMemoryTaskStore CRUD operations to CLI invocation via subprocess. Tests validate: (1) a Task can be created, stored, retrieved, updated, and deleted through InMemoryTaskStore in a single workflow; (2) CLI add/list/update/delete commands produce correct stdout/stderr output and exit codes when invoked as 'python -m tasklib.cli'; (3) error paths (empty title, duplicate add, get/update/delete non-existent id) produce correct exceptions or non-zero CLI exit codes; (4) public API imports from tasklib root work as documented. All tests use only unittest from the standard library.
